location.office_time_required: hybrid — time split between office/workshops and home, no fixed percentage stated

secondary_archetype: Supply Chain / Hardware Operations Program Manager

level_signals: 10+ years of progressive experience in supply chain program management
Compensation range $184,200–$240,000 base consistent with senior IC
End-to-end program ownership including cross-functional and external partner management

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of progressive experience in supply chain program management, NPI, and hardware operations
Strong understanding of BOM management, material planning, and procurement processes.
Demonstrated ability to manage cross-functional programs across Engineering, Operations, and Supply Chain.
Familiarity with supplier compliance frameworks including TISAX, ISO standards, and ethical sourcing requirements.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and risk management skills.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management capabilities.
Ability to travel internationally as needed

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Automotive, consumer, hardware, or robotic industry experience preferred

scope.surface: End-to-end supply chain execution across assigned programs and product builds — prototype development, NPI, and fleet launches
scope.business_model: B2B — AI/autonomous driving software and hardware sold to automakers
core_responsibilities: Act as the primary Supply Chain lead for assigned programs or internal product builds (e.g., prototypes, new product introduction, subassembly)
Own end-to-end material readiness to support program milestones across R&D, Engineering, Operations, and Manufacturing
Translate engineering BOMs and aggregate Technical Program Manager demand into procurement plans
Identify long-lead items and critical path components early in the product lifecycle
Collaborating with Supply Chain Team Leads to ensure direct purchasing, indirect purchasing, and logistics suppliers are meeting compliance requirements
Serve as the primary program manager for upfitter and partner relationships, ensuring alignment across all stakeholders
Define, track, and report on key supply chain performance metrics including, material availability, total cost saving, inventory cycle count accuracy

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($184,200–$240,000 base) plus equity
Clear role scope — end-to-end supply chain ownership for hardware programs
Well-defined cross-functional partners and responsibilities listed
Hybrid working policy with core hours giving schedule flexibility
Company encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every requirement
Explicit diversity and inclusion commitments stated
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international candidates
International travel required with no frequency specified — ambiguous commitment level
10+ years minimum is a high bar that may filter strong candidates with 7–9 years of directly relevant experience
Role spans US, UK, and other regions but is based only in Sunnyvale, CA — significant timezone burden implied

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from compensation range and years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — AI software/hardware for automakers)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (partially inferred; some metrics stated in JD under KPI section)
ambiguities: Supplier compliance frameworks (TISAX, ISO) listed under 'Skills & Qualifications' without explicit 'required' vs 'preferred' language — treated as hard requirement given unconditional bullet phrasing, but could be interpreted as preferred domain knowledge
International travel frequency not specified — 'as needed' leaves scope undefined
JD does not specify whether visa sponsorship is available for this role
